title: CubeSat 3-Axis Cold-Gas RCS Attitude Hold System
description: A closed-loop, three-axis attitude hold controller for a 1U CubeSat using six paired cold-gas solenoid thrusters. An ICM-42688-P IMU provides high-rate gyro and accelerometer data over SPI. A cascaded PID (outer attitude / inner rate) computes per-axis torque commands mapped to thruster pulse widths via a three-axis RCS allocation scheme. A fault-safe state machine monitors IMU heartbeat, tank pressure, and solenoid duty cycle, commanding a SAFE_MODE inhibit on any anomaly. All states, sensor readings, and actuator commands are logged over UART as structured CSV telemetry. Differentiated from the existing Adaptive Controller by its multi-axis thruster allocation architecture, hardware solenoid driver with interlock, and structured fault-tagged telemetry.
objective: Demonstrate closed-loop 3-axis attitude hold to within ±2 deg/s angular rate error, with sub-second fault detection, safe-mode inhibit of all thrusters, and continuous UART telemetry log at 50 Hz.
constraints: Arduino Mega or STM32 Blue Pill MCU; six IRLZ44N MOSFET drivers and 12 V solenoid valves; ICM-42688-P IMU on SPI; analog pressure transducer on ADC pin; solenoid duty cycle ≤5%; tank pressure floor 50 kPa before SAFE_MODE; Factor of Safety ≥1.5 on propellant reserve; total BOM < $40.
supporting_fact_titles: Cold-Gas Thruster Dynamics and Pulse-Width Modulation Control
Three-Axis RCS Thruster Allocation and Torque Mapping
design_element_titles: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
UART Telemetry Logger with Fault Tagging

element_usage_notes.ICM-42688-P High-Rate IMU via SPI: Polled at 50 Hz over SPI to provide gyro (deg/s) and rate-integrated attitude estimates on all three body axes. IMU heartbeat timeout >500 ms triggers SAFE_MODE.
element_usage_notes.Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controlle: Outer loop tracks attitude setpoint (0,0,0 for hold); inner loop tracks gyro rate. Output is a signed torque command per axis fed to the thruster allocation layer.
element_usage_notes.Fault-Safe Propulsion State Machine: Manages INIT→ARMED→CONTROLLING→SAFE_MODE transitions. SAFE_MODE de-energises all six solenoid channels and latches the fault LED. Only a power cycle resets from SAFE_MODE.
element_usage_notes.Six-Channel Cold-Gas Solenoid Driver (MO: Receives signed per-axis commands; activates the appropriate +/- MOSFET with a pulse width proportional to |cmd|*dt, capped at 20 ms. Hardware interlock ensures opposing valves cannot open simultaneously.
element_usage_notes.UART Telemetry Logger with Fault Tagging: Emits a CSV line every control cycle. DATA lines carry timestamp, attitude, commands, and pressure. FAULT lines carry the fault code and state transition. Used for post-flight analysis and real-time ground monitoring.

element_interactions.to: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.data: gx, gy, gz (deg/s) + integrated attX, attY, attZ (deg)
element_interactions.description: IMU gyro readings feed the inner rate loop; integrated angles feed the outer attitude loop of the cascaded PID.

element_interactions.from: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: Per-axis signed commands are translated into MOSFET PWM pulse widths by the thruster allocation layer inside the driver element.
On SAFE_MODE entry the state machine immediately clears all six MOSFET drive pins, cutting thruster power regardless of pending PID commands.

element_interactions.to: UART Telemetry Logger with Fault Tagging
element_interactions.data: state transition code + fault reason string
element_interactions.description: Every state transition emits a FAULT-tagged CSV line so ground operators can correlate anomalies with telemetry context.

element_interactions.to: UART Telemetry Logger with Fault Tagging
element_interactions.description: Actuator commands and tank pressure are included in every DATA telemetry line for propellant budget accounting.
